currentFocus: PatchBenchLaunchPresetText presentation helper and consult evidence contract are accepted on main; the next developer slice is optional PatchBench page-routing test hardening or primitive projection design before receipt/source-summary extraction.

integratedWave.summary: Accepted on main at 04e03e92, the canonical integration accepts the music live-bundle readiness hardening, PatchBenchLaunchPresetText presentation extraction, and consult evidence reporting contract while preserving storage no-delete truths.
integratedWave.patchBenchMusicStatus: BinaryPatchesPage music replacement status-line assignments now route through PatchBenchSafeCopyOutcomeText for default, not-ready, progress, staged, restore, and failure states. The helper remains presentation-only and accepts primitive string/bool state.
integratedWave.patchBenchLaunchPresetText: BinaryPatchesPage launch/control preset automation names and status-message text now route through PatchBenchLaunchPresetText. The helper remains presentation-only; launch option tuples, selection state, matching logic, launch argument construction, safe-copy launch planning, service calls, process control, patch/catalog semantics, Host/Join/online claims, and runtime proof boundaries remain in BinaryPatchesPage.
integratedWave.tests: PatchBenchSafeCopyOutcomeTextTests and PatchBenchLaunchPresetTextTests assert exact reflected helper output for moved presentation strings. WinUiProductLaneTests guards page-to-helper routing, helper behavior boundaries, and absence of prior inline status literals.
integratedWave.consultReportContract: coordination/REPORT_CONTRACT.md now requires explicit Consult Evidence entries for mandated Codex specialist, Codex adversarial, cursor-agent composer-2.5-fast, and cursor-agent gemini-3.1-pro lanes, including accepted/rejected findings and CONSULT_UNAVAILABLE reasons without raw prompt/log/local-path leakage.
integratedWave.adversarialReviewDisposition: PatchBench and storage/consult adversarial reviews accepted integration with P3-only notes: optional future launch-preset page-routing/static guard hardening, retained-pool GiB overclaim avoidance, future deletion manifest rigor, music pre-arm wording clarity, and future consult-model maintenance.
integratedWave.storagePolicy: No tracked storage policy edit was needed; existing LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY and coordination policy already cover campaign scratch disposal, scratch-volume non-durability, and manifest/classification requirements for Ghidra/proof pruning.

musicLiveBundleReadiness.gateTruth: Producer coverage can be complete, but the public gate reports readyToRunLiveAttempt=false, liveArmAllowed=false, runtimeAudibleOutputProof=false, and preArmReadiness.status=prearm-readiness-not-proven.
musicLiveBundleReadiness.executorTruth: The armed executor requires a private accepted winui-safe-copy-music-audible-output-live-bundle-prearm-readiness.v1 JSON through --prearm-readiness-json before live delegation. That private artifact can authorize a live attempt only after real pre-arm authority and readiness checks; it is not runtime audible-output proof by itself.
musicLiveBundleReadiness.statusLock: Top-level gate status is value-locked to producer-coverage-complete-prearm-readiness-required after adversarial correction.
musicLiveBundleReadiness.followupScope: Read-only follow-up adds no runtime proof. Future prompts must treat producerCoverageComplete as schema/producer coverage only, pre-arm permission as permission to attempt only, public gate root arguments as non-proof, and executor receipts/stdout as private artifacts.
musicLiveBundleReadiness.nonClaims: No BEA launch, CDB attach, audio capture, copied executable mutation, installed-game/original-BEA mutation, runtime audible-output proof, gameplay behavior, runtime causality, online readiness, release readiness, rebuild parity, or no-noticeable-difference proof exists from this wave.

storageCloseout.inventory: Storage inventory was partial-bounded and metadata-only, not deletion authorization.
storageCloseout.scratchCleanup: Safe scratch/build/worktree cleanup candidate pool was effectively 0 GiB for this wave.
storageCloseout.ghidraBackups: Ghidra backup retention deleted 0 files and retained all reviewed Ghidra backup/live/unknown rows; the reviewed backup pool was about 142.295 GiB and remained blocked by hard-retain markers, ambiguous family taxonomy, missing supersession proof, and absent deletion approval.
storageCloseout.proofArchives: Proof archive retention deleted 0 files and retained all reviewed proof-related rows; the reviewed proof-unknown pool was about 0.565 GiB and remained blocked by partial metadata-only inventory and missing stale/duplicate/supersession proof.
storageCloseout.gitMetadata: Stale local Git administrative worktree metadata cleanup pruned 23 stale metadata records after dry-run evidence. It deleted no branches, remote refs, physical directories, tracked source, final reports, proof/Ghidra/game/release material, hard payloads, or secrets.
storageCloseout.currentWaveDeletionClassification: Current high-confidence deletion candidates: 0 rows. Current authorized reclaimed storage: 0 GiB. No storage deletion or mutation was performed in this wave; retained Ghidra/proof GiB values are approximate review-pool metrics only, not unique reclaimable storage.
storageCloseout.futureLocalMaintenance: Ghidra/proof family taxonomy, proof manifest/hash comparison, branch cleanup, and physical scratch cleanup remain separate local maintenance tasks requiring explicit scope, dry-run evidence, and deletion authority.

currentTruth.patchBenchBoundary: PatchBenchSafeCopyOutcomeText remains presentation-only. BinaryPatchesPage still owns service calls, UI control assignment, operation logs, AppStatusService status, AppCore receipt construction, patch planning, safe-copy manifests/signatures, launch arguments, process decisions, patch catalog rows, byte data, music behavior, Host/Join availability, online readiness, runtime proof, and release packaging are unchanged.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.futureRuntimeRecommendation: Music live-bundle tooling remains fail-closed. Any future private live attempt must stop unless explicit authority, leases, an empty isolated proof root, no preexisting BEA/CDB processes, and capture span/flush/decode-window readiness are proven by the private pre-arm artifact and executor path.
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, screenshots/frame dumps, raw proof logs, copied executables, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env* files, build output, package output, local proof paths, raw manifests, and local runtime caches stay out of git and release ZIPs.

consultCompliance: Scoped only: inspected recent June 29 non-trivial worker outputs had no clear skipped Cursor lane; REPORT_CONTRACT now requires explicit per-lane Consult Evidence for mandated Codex and Cursor consults, but this remains a reporting-contract improvement rather than a blanket campaign-wide compliance claim.

activeSlice.name: patchbench-launch-preset-text-and-consult-contract
activeSlice.goal: PatchBenchLaunchPresetText / consult-contract wave is accepted on main at 04e03e92; PatchBenchLaunchPresetText extracts launch/control preset automation names and status-message text as a pure presentation helper, and consult report evidence requirements are explicit in REPORT_CONTRACT.
activeSlice.claimBoundary: presentation-only WinUI helper and coordination documentation only; BinaryPatchesPage still owns launch option tuples, selection state, matching logic, launch argument construction, safe-copy launch planning, service calls, process control, patch/catalog semantics, Host/Join/online claims, and runtime proof boundaries. No storage deletion boundary changed.

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

validation.currentIntegration: Integration validation uses focused PatchBench WinUI/AppCore-safe gates, JSON parse for edited state files, git diff --check, docs/repo safety gates, hard-payload safety, and public allowlist checks.
validation.nextSlice: Use focused WinUI helper-output/static tests for any optional PatchBenchLaunchPresetText page-routing hardening. Do not run live BEA/CDB/audio proof unless a later prompt explicitly grants runtime-proof authority, leases, arm phrase, private proof root, and stop conditions.

nextSteps: Before the next write, verify live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main.
Optionally harden PatchBenchLaunchPresetText page-routing/literal reintroduction tests if future work touches the launch preset surface.
Design a primitive projection contract before any receipt/source-summary helper extraction; keep behavior and AppCore receipts in the page/AppCore owners until then.
Use the next write for focused PatchBench test hardening or primitive projection design, not more closeout bookkeeping for this completed wave.

Keep package scripts, release publication, Host/Join, online readiness, runtime audio proof, gameplay, visual, rebuild, no-noticeable-difference, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, original-BEA, proof archive deletion, and raw private-path claims bounded to accepted evidence.
Treat Ghidra/proof taxonomy, proof manifest/hash comparison, branch cleanup, and physical scratch cleanup as separate local maintenance work requiring explicit authority.
